conversations.content:
I'll be arriving in London on March 15th. Last year, I stayed at the 'London Luxury Hotel', which had amazing reviews and ratings. It was super quiet and clean, and my room had a view of the Thames River. It was such a great experience! This time, I want to try out a new hotel. Once I figure out which one is the best, I'll head there and then find a good restaurant to eat at with my friends.
I have a budget of 160 for the hotel, and I want to stay in a hotel with the highest rating. Can you recommend a hotel? Tell me the name, rating, price range, and address. Thanks a bunch!

<function_thought>
I need to start by retrieving the list of all hotels in London. This will allow me to find hotels that fit the user's criteria of having a budget of 160. Once I have the list of hotels, I'll determine their prices, ratings, and addresses to recommend the one with the highest rating within the budget. Therefore, the first step is to execute the `get_all_hotels_in_city` function for London.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_all_hotels_in_city(city="London")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

Hotel Names: Cozy Stay
Mom's Home
London Luxury
Covent Garden Retreat
Riverside View

<function_thought>
I have retrieved the list of hotels in London: "Cozy Stay", "Mom's Home", "London Luxury", "Covent Garden Retreat", and "Riverside View". The next step is to get the price range for these hotels to find one within the user's budget of 160. Therefore, I will execute the `get_hotels_prices` function using the names of these hotels.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_hotels_prices(hotel_names=["Cozy Stay", "Mom's Home", "London Luxury", "Covent Garden Retreat", "Riverside View"])]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

Next, I need to get the ratings and reviews for these hotels to find the one with the highest rating. Thus, I will execute the `get_rating_reviews_for_hotels` function with these hotel names.
